item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd like to see a tale about how the employees somehow fail at being a human sacrifice. Maybe they poison some deity, lol.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is great. I would love to read a tale from the point of view of a regular employee at PIC99, dealing with the strange antics of the other “employees”.

However, I’m not keen on the idea that the foundation is able to use the bodies of the dead 5902-A instances as regular human corpses. It seems far too useful. It would be better if they just demanifested shortly after death, similar to how they respond if they are fired. It’s implied that they aren’t actually real, and just extensions of the administration department, so them leaving a corpse behind doesn’t make much sense to me.

Apart from that small issue, very good article.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I put this one off for far too long. Thoroughly entertaining and incredibly fun.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really enjoyed it, the photo of SCP-5902-PRIME is also great

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As others have mentioned, the clinical tone isn't quite perfect, and I'd recommend taking the advice from Christopoulos, but it doesn't really effect the article too much, all things considered.

I especially like the bit about the -A instances being used for human sacrifices! That's exactly the kind of creative thinking that the foundation would apply. After all, these things are far more expendable than real humans.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Excellent article, but I can't for the life of me figure out the significance of the door mat.

…There isn't any, is there?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A good reading, the mental image that causes me is funny. I think in those things as bureaucratic parasites. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
this is very well written and very enjoyable. and, with less fervency, i agree with SirStafford; a hub for the dept of administration and whatever nonsense they get up to would be very enjoyable if written as well as this article. i'd love to see them disrupting and/or bumbling their way through a complicated plot.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Cant wait for the Dept. Of Admin Hub.

Not kidding. Get cracking.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The description drags and is much longer than it ought to be. Everything from the security camera log onwards is great though, and just about manages to redeem the article for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Man, I can't believe replacing all that wiring with ham didn't work, I was certain it'd increase productivity threefold.

+1 you silly silly men

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i love how the foundation's solution to this is a bureaucratically mandated analogue to drowning your sims in a swimming pool

very fun read

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gold. I love homonculus Abbot and Costello. Want to see them do Who's On First, but pretending to be census takers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ My thoughts
-My thoughts

a very large building with thousands of employees may allow a 5902-A to remain unnoticed and “multiply”. Through a currently unknown process, one 5902-A left in place will lead to more appearing. “Appear” is used literally here -

The quotes on "multiply" are unnecessary, and the "is used literally here" part is super non-clinical.

902-A do not possess the experience, knowledge, intelligence, or apparently even the desire necessary for the administration of any property they acquire.

Way too much emphasis on "desire" for this to me remotely clinical. At minimum, drop the italics. Maybe drop the "even" as well.

+1 regardless. The doormat part killed me.

item.title: you have nothing to business but business itself
item.content:encoded:
This was a lot of fun! I only really wrote the containment procedures though, and the rest of it is really… none of my business.

𝐌𝐚𝐥𝐢𝐜𝐞𝐀𝐟𝐨𝐫𝐞𝐭𝐡𝐨𝐮𝐠𝐡𝐭 holds no responsibility for any practices or consideration thereof which may not or otherwise pertain to outcomes or in effect allow such outcomes to persist. 𝐌𝐚𝐥𝐢𝐜𝐞𝐀𝐟𝐨𝐫𝐞𝐭𝐡𝐨𝐮𝐠𝐡𝐭 expresses no opinions or opinions thereupon, nor in permitting statements considers statements to be the opinion of 𝐌𝐚𝐥𝐢𝐜𝐞𝐀𝐟𝐨𝐫𝐞𝐭𝐡𝐨𝐮𝐠𝐡𝐭 or associated parties or parties thus opined in or upon through association. In the event of events, actions enacted or effected to affect events or others may be considered to be considered for the duration of this or by association any other event or statement of events.

item.title: this is a routine inspection, ma'am
item.content:encoded:
i wrote a scp article haha

but i didn't write it alone, MaliceAforethought wrote some of it too. we did it… TOGETHER. so blame him instead of me.

enjoy. ♥

EDIT: the inestimable etoisle has drawn a picture of our professionals being very effective and adequate at the job site. give them all of your money and land.
